title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: passt bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for passt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: passt implements a translation layer between a Layer-2 network interface and native Layer-4 sockets (TCP, UDP, ICMP/ICMPv6 echo) on a host. It doesn't require any capabilities or privileges, and it can be used as a simple replacement for Slirp.  pasta (same binary as passt, different command) offers equivalent functionality, for network namespaces: traffic is forwarded using a tap interface inside the namespace, without the need to create further interfaces on the host, hence not requiring any capabilities or privileges.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): passt/pasta can crash on certain sequences of out of order TCP packets [rhel-10.0.z] (JIRA:RHEL-123414)  passt might truncate data transfers if socket buffer fills up right as we receive FIN from container/guest [rhel-10.0.z] (JIRA:RHEL-123421)

full_text:
An update for passt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

passt implements a translation layer between a Layer-2 network interface and native Layer-4 sockets (TCP, UDP, ICMP/ICMPv6 echo) on a host. It doesn't require any capabilities or privileges, and it can be used as a simple replacement for Slirp.  pasta (same binary as passt, different command) offers equivalent functionality, for network namespaces: traffic is forwarded using a tap interface inside the namespace, without the need to create further interfaces on the host, hence not requiring any capabilities or privileges.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): passt/pasta can crash on certain sequences of out of order TCP packets [rhel-10.0.z] (JIRA:RHEL-123414)  passt might truncate data transfers if socket buffer fills up right as we receive FIN from container/guest [rhel-10.0.z] (JIRA:RHEL-123421)
